title: देवी अपराध क्षमापन स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री देवी अपराधक्षमापन स्तोत्रम् ॥

न मन्त्रं नो यन्त्रं तदपि च न जाने स्तुतिमहो
न चाह्वानं ध्यानं तदपि च न जाने स्तुतिकथाः।
न जाने मुद्रिष्टे तदपि च न जाने विलपनं
परं जाने मातस्त्वदनुसरणं क्लेशहरणम्॥१॥

विधेरज्ञानेन द्रविणविरहेणालसतया
विधेयाशक्यत्वात्तव चरणयोर्या च्युतिरभूत्।
तदेतत्क्षन्तव्यं जननि सकलोद्धारिणि शिवे
कुपुत्रो जायेत क्वचिदपि कुमाता न भवति॥२॥

पृथिव्यां पुत्रास्ते जननि बहवः सन्ति सरलाः
परं तेषां मध्ये विरलतरलोऽहं तव सुतः।
मदीयोऽयं त्यागः समुचितमिदं नो तव शिवे
कुपुत्रो जायेत क्वचिदपि कुमाता न भवति॥३॥

जगन्मातर्मातस्तव चरणसेवा न रचिता
न वा दत्तं देवि द्रविणमपि भूयस्तव मया।
तथापि त्वं स्नेहं मयि निरुपमं यत्प्रकुरुषे
कुपुत्रो जायेत क्वचिदपि कुमाता न भवति॥४॥

परित्यक्ता देवा विविधविधिसेवाकुलतया
मया पञ्चाशीतेरधिकमपनीते तु वयसि।
इदानीं चेन्मातस्तव यदि कृपा नापि भविता
निरालम्बो लम्बोदरजननि कं यामि शरणम्॥५॥

श्वपाको जल्पाको भवति मधुपाकोपमगिरा
निरातङ्को रंको विहरति चिरं कोटिकनकैः।
तवापर्णे कर्णे विशति मनुवर्णे फलमिदं
जनः को जानीते जननि जपनीयं जपविधौ॥६॥

चिताभस्मालेपो गरलमशनं दिक्पटधरो
जटाधारी कण्ठे भुजगपतिहारी पशुपतिः।
कपाली भूतेशो भजति जगदीशैकपदवीं
भवानि त्वत्पाणिग्रहणपरिपाटीफलमिदम्॥७॥

न मोक्षस्याकाङ्क्षा भवविभववाञ्छापि च न मे
न विज्ञानापेक्षा शशिमुखि सुखेच्छापि न पुनः।
अतस्त्वां संयाचे जननि जननं यातु मम वै
मृडानी रुद्राणी शिव शिव भवानीति जपतः॥८॥

नाराधितासि विधिना विविधोपचारैः
किं रूक्षचिन्तनपरैर्न कृतं वचोभिः।
श्यामे त्वमेव यदि किञ्चन मय्यनाथे
धत्से कृपामुचितमम्ब परं तवैव॥९॥

आपत्सु मग्नः स्मरणं त्वदीयं करोमि दुर्गे करुणार्णवेशि।
नैतच्छठत्वं मम भावयेथाः क्षुधातृषार्ता जननीं स्मरन्ति॥१०॥

जगदम्ब विचित्रमत्र किं परिपूर्णा करुणास्ति चेन्मयि।
अपराधपरम्परावृतं न हि माता समुपेक्षते सुतम्॥११॥

मत्समः पातकी नास्ति पापघ्नी त्वत्समा न हि।
एवं ज्ञात्वा महादेवि यथायोग्यं तथा कुरु॥१२॥

॥ इति श्रीमच्छङ्कराचार्यविरचितं देवी अपराधक्षमापन स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Devi Aparadha-kshamapana Stotram ||

Na Mantram No Yantram Tadapi Cha Na Jane Stutimaho
Na Chahvanam Dhyanam Tadapi Cha Na Jane Stuti-kathah.
Na Jane Mudrishte Tadapi Cha Na Jane Vilapanam
Param Jane Matas-tvad-anusaranam Klesha-haranam.||1||

Vidher Ajnanena Dravina-virahena Alasataya
Vidheyashakya-tvat Tava Charanyor Ya Chyutir Abhut.
Tad Etat Kshantavyam Janani Sakalod-dharini Shive
Kuputro Jayeta Kvachid Api Kumata Na Bhavati.||2||

Prithivyam Putras Te Janani Bahavah Santi Saralah
Param Tesham Madhye Viralataral-o'ham Tava Sutah.
Madiyo'yam Tyagah Samuchitam Idam No Tava Shive
Kuputro Jayeta Kvachid Api Kumata Na Bhavati.||3||

Jagan-matar Matas Tava Charana-seva Na Rachita
Na Va Dattam Devi Dravinam Api Bhuyas Tava Maya.
Tathapi Tvam Sneham Mayi Nirupamam Yat Prakurushe
Kuputro Jayeta Kvachid Api Kumata Na Bhavati.||4||

Parityakta Deva Vividha-vidhi-seva-kulataya
Maya Panchashiter Adhikam Apanite Tu Vayasi.
Idanim Chen Matas Tava Yadi Kripa Napi Bhavita
Niralambho Lambodara-janani Kam Yami Sharanam.||5||

Shvapako Jalpako Bhavati Madhupako-pama-gira
Niratanko Ranko Viharati Chiram Koti-kanakaih.
Tavaparne Karne Vishati Manu-varne Phalam Idam
Janah Ko Janite Janani Japaniyam Japa-vidhau.||6||

Chita-bhasma-lepo Garalam Ashanam Dikpata-dharo
Jata-dhari Kanthe Bhujaga-pati-hari Pashupatih.
Kapali Bhutesho Bhajati Jagad-ishaika-padavim
Bhavani Tvat-pani-grahana-paripati-phalam Idam.||7||

Na Mokshasyakanksha Bhava-vibhava-vanchha Api Cha Na Me
Na Vijnanapeksha Shashimukhi Sukhe-chchha Api Na Punah.
Atas Tvam Samyache Janani Jananam Yatu Mama Vai
Mridani Rudrani Shiva Shiva Bhavani-iti Japatah.||8||

Naradhitasi Vidhina Vividho-pacharaih
Kim Ruksha-chintana-parair Na Kritam Vachobhih.
Shyame Tvam Eva Yadi Kinchana Mayy Anathe
Dhatse Kripam Uchitam Amba Param Tavaiva.||9||

Aapatsu Magnah Smaranam Tvadiyam Karomi Durge Karuna-arnave-shi.
Naitatchchhathhatvam Mama Bhavayethah Kshudhaa-trisharta Jananim Smaranti.||10||

Jagadamba Vichitram Atra Kim Paripurna Karuna-asti Chen Mayi.
Aparadha-parampara-avritam Na Hi Mata Samupekshate Sutam.||11||

Matsamah Pataki Nasti Papaghni Tvatsama Na Hi.
Evam Jnatva Mahadevi Yathayogyam Tatha Kuru.||12||

|| Iti Shrimach-Shankaracharya-virachitam Devi Aparadha-kshamapana Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
मुझे न मन्त्र आता है, न यन्त्र; न स्तुति जानता हूँ, न आह्वान, न ध्यान; न मुद्रा जानता हूँ, न विलाप करना; परन्तु हे माता! इतना जानता हूँ कि तुम्हारा अनुसरण क्लेशों को हरने वाला है।

विधि के अज्ञान से, धन के अभाव से, आलस्य से, और असमर्थता से तुम्हारे चरणों से जो च्युति हुई, हे जननि! उसे क्षमा करो। कुपुत्र हो सकता है, किन्तु कुमाता कभी नहीं होती।

हे जगन्माता! मैंने न तुम्हारे चरणों की सेवा की, न धन अर्पित किया। फिर भी तुम मुझ पर अनुपम स्नेह करती हो। कुपुत्र हो सकता है, किन्तु कुमाता कभी नहीं होती।

मुझसे बड़ा पापी कोई नहीं और तुमसे बड़ी पापनाशिनी कोई नहीं। हे महादेवी! यह जानकर जो उचित हो वह करो।
benefits: Invokes forgiveness for all offenses committed against the divine mother
Invokes the unconditional motherly love and compassion of Devi
Traditionally recited for removal of guilt and spiritual burden from past mistakes
Deepens the devotional bond between devotee and the Goddess
Invokes the blessing of divine grace even upon the most fallen souls
One of Shankaracharya's most moving and heartfelt compositions
description: The Devi Aparadha Kshamapana Stotram, known in Sanskrit as देवी अपराध क्षमापन स्तोत्रम्, is a devotional hymn of praise in praise of Goddess Durga. This sacred composition is sourced from Composed by Adi Shankaracharya and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Regular recitation is believed to invoke the unconditional motherly love and compassion of Devi, bestow divine grace even upon the most fallen souls, and deepen the devotional bond between devotee and the Goddess. For best results, devotees chant this on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Navratri. With 12 verses and a reading time of about 8 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Devi Aparadha Kshamapana Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Devi Aparadha Kshamapana Stotram by Shankaracharya in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Prayer seeking forgiveness from the Divine Mother.
relatedSearches: Devi Aparadha Kshamapana stotram lyrics Sanskrit
Kuputro jayet kvachidapi kumata na bhavati verse

seoFaqs.question: What is the central message of the Devi Aparadha Kshamapana Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The stotram's recurring theme is the absolute compassion of the Divine Mother, expressed through the famous line that even if a son turns out wayward (kuputro jayeta kvachidapi), a mother (kumata) never can. Adi Shankaracharya structures the hymn around frank confessions of failure: lapses in mantra, tantra, worship procedure, posture, dress and concentration, all admitted before Jagadamba. The poet then argues that the mother's grace is precisely what the imperfect devotee needs, since flawless worshippers do not require kshamapana. As Shlokam.org and SanatanaVeda summarise, the central rasa is vatsalya (maternal affection received by an erring child), making this one of the most emotionally direct of Shankara's compositions.

seoFaqs.question: When is the Devi Aparadha Kshamapana Stotram traditionally recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The stotram is most often chanted at the conclusion of any Devi puja, parayana or stotra recitation, as a formal kshamapana for errors of pronunciation, sequence, materials or attention during the rite. As TemplePurohit and SanatanaVeda explain, no Shakta puja is considered complete without an aparadha-kshamapana, and Shankara's hymn is the most widely used among Devi worshippers. It is also recited at the end of Durga Saptashati paath, Lalita Sahasranama parayana and Khadgamala recitation. Beyond ritual closure, many devotees include it in their daily Devi sandhya as a humility practice, since its tone of confession is considered purifying even when no specific puja has been performed.

seoFaqs.question: Which famous verse from this stotram is most quoted?
seoFaqs.answer: The most cited verse is: 'Kuputro jayeta kvachidapi kumata na bhavati' (a son may sometimes be unworthy, but a mother is never unworthy). The verse continues that even if Devi has thousands of good sons, none compares in helplessness to this one supplicant, and so she should not abandon him. Shlokam.org reproduces this verse with English commentary noting that Shankara deliberately sets up an asymmetric relationship: the devotee can fail the mother, but the mother cannot fail the child. This single shloka is so often quoted in Shakta literature that some devotees memorise it in isolation as a daily prayer of refuge, even when they do not recite the full stotram.

seoFaqs.question: Is this stotram only for Goddess Durga, or any form of Devi?
seoFaqs.answer: Although the address Jagadamba is sometimes associated specifically with Durga, Adi Shankaracharya wrote the hymn with a deliberately universal vocative so it can be offered to any form of the Devi. SanatanaVeda's commentary points out that the verses use generic mother-imagery (Amba, Mata, Jagadamba) rather than form-specific epithets, which is why it serves as the standard kshamapana after worship of Durga, Lalita Tripura Sundari, Bhuvaneshwari, Kali, Bhagavati and other forms. In Sri Vidya parampara it is recited after Khadgamala or Lalita Sahasranama; in Bengal it follows Durga Saptashati; in South Indian temples it is chanted after deity-specific stotras. The stotram's flexibility is precisely why it has become pan-Shakta.

seoFaqs.question: What kinds of mistakes does the stotram explicitly ask forgiveness for?
seoFaqs.answer: Shankara enumerates three broad categories: errors in mantra (mispronunciation, missed syllables, broken meter), errors in kriya (omitted upacharas, wrong materials, broken sequence), and errors in bhakti (wandering mind, lack of devotion, bodily restlessness during worship). He goes further to admit that the devotee may not even know all the proper procedures (na mantram no yantram tadapi cha na jane stuthimaho) and offers the worship anyway, trusting the mother's grace to fill the gaps. As TemplePurohit summarises, this comprehensive aparadha framing is what makes the stotram universally applicable, since it covers virtually every possible defect in any ritual context, and reframes inadequacy as the very ground for the mother's compassionate response.

seoFaqs.question: How does this stotram differ from the Shiva Aparadha Kshamapana Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Both are companion compositions by Adi Shankaracharya, structurally parallel but theologically distinct. The Shiva Aparadha Kshamapana, hosted on Shlokam.org alongside the Devi version, addresses Mahadeva and emphasises the absolute lord's authority to forgive transgressions of dharma, drawing on father-and-king imagery. The Devi version uses mother-and-child imagery and rests its argument on vatsalya (motherly love that does not depend on the child's worthiness). The Shiva stotram is recited after Shiva worship (Rudrabhisheka, Bilva archana, Lingashtakam), while the Devi stotram is recited after any Shakta puja. Together they exemplify Shankara's pattern of pairing a Shaiva and a Shakta liturgical aid for every major ritual occasion.
